rows.title: India's IRDAI Information and Cyber Security Guidelines, 2026
rows.summary: IRDAI Information and Cyber Security Guidelines, 2026 — superseding the 2023 framework. Issued via Circular IRDAI/GA&HR/CIR/MISC/51/4/2026 dated 6 April 2026. Effective FY 2026-27. Binds all insurers, intermediaries (FRBs, brokers, corporate agents, web aggregators, TPAs, IMFs, repositories, ISNPs, corporate surveyors, MISPs, CSCs) and the IIB. Key changes vs 2023: quarterly ISRMC meetings, abolition of CMC, new IT Steering Committee under CTO, mandatory Independent External Expert on RMC, DPDP Act 2023 compliance now mandatory, tiered exceptions framework. Annexure A summarises changes; Annexure B is the full guidelines. Bilingual (Hindi/English) original. Hindi title: आईआरडीएआई सूचना और साइबर सुरक्षा दिशानिर्देश, 2026.
authorities: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India
rows.authority: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

rows.title: Open letter issued to tech firms to strengthen age checks and protect children’s data
rows.summary: The Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) has today published an open letter to social media and video‑sharing platforms operating in the UK, calling on them to strengthen age assurance measures so young children can’t access services that are not designed for them.

rows.title: Philippines NPC Accountability Agent Recognition under APEC and Global CBPR Systems
rows.summary: NPC programme for recognising Accountability Agents under both the APEC Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR) System and the Global CBPR System. Accountability Agents certify whether participating organisations meet CBPR requirements and work collaboratively with companies, consumers, and governments on cross-border data transfer accountability. Separate applications are required for APEC and Global CBPR — recognition under one does not constitute recognition under the other. Topics: Privacy, cross-border data transfers, certification.

rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «02 Guía práctica y ejemplos para entender el Reglamento de IA». Practical guide with worked examples illustrating EU AI Act application across use cases, risk categories, and stakeholder roles.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 08 — Transparency
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «08 Guía Transparencia». Guidance on transparency obligations for high-risk AI systems and certain general-purpose AI under EU AI Act Articles 13 and 50, covering instructions for use and downstream-deployer information.

rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 03 — Conformity Assessment
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «03 Guía Evaluación de conformidad». Guidance on conformity assessment procedures for high-risk AI systems under EU AI Act Article 43, covering both internal control and notified-body pathways.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial
rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 13 — Post-Market Monitoring
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «13 Guía Vigilancia poscomercialización». Guidance on post-market monitoring obligations for providers of high-risk AI systems under EU AI Act Article 72, covering monitoring system design, data collection, and corrective action.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial
rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 04 — Quality Management System
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «04 Guía del sistema de gestión de la calidad». Guidance on the quality management system providers of high-risk AI systems must implement under EU AI Act Article 17.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 07 — Data and Data Governance
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «07 Guía de datos y gobernanza de datos». Guidance on data and data governance obligations for high-risk AI systems under EU AI Act Article 10, covering training, validation, and testing data quality, representativeness, and bias mitigation.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial
rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 15 — Technical Documentation
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «15 Guía Documentación Técnica». Guidance on the technical documentation that providers of high-risk AI systems must compile under EU AI Act Article 11 and Annex IV, including system description, design, monitoring, and validation.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial
rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 11 — Cybersecurity
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «11 Guía Ciberseguridad». Guidance on cybersecurity obligations for high-risk AI systems under EU AI Act Article 15, covering protection against unauthorised use, data poisoning, model evasion, and confidentiality attacks.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial
rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 06 — Human Oversight
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «06 Guía Vigilancia humana». Guidance on human oversight obligations for high-risk AI systems under EU AI Act Article 14, covering oversight design, operator capabilities, and intervention measures.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial
rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 09 — Accuracy
rows.summary: Verbatim source title (es): «09 Guía de Precisión». Guidance on accuracy obligations for high-risk AI systems under EU AI Act Article 15, covering metric selection, declared performance levels, and ongoing monitoring.
authorities: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial
rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: Spain's AESIA Guide 01 — Introduction to the EU AI Act

rows.authority: Agencia Espanola de Supervision de la Inteligencia Artificial

rows.title: India's AI Governance Guidelines (November 2025)
rows.summary: 68-page voluntary AI governance framework released 4 November 2025 by MeitY. Drafted by an expert committee constituted in July 2025; chaired by Prof. Balaraman Ravindran (IIT Madras), lead writer Amlan Mohanty (NITI Aayog). Members: Rahul Matthan (Trilegal), Debjani Ghosh (NITI Aayog), Kalika Bali (IndiaAI), Sharad Sharma (iSPIRT), Abhishek Singh (AS, MeitY), Kavita Bhatia (Scientist G, MeitY/IndiaAI COO), Abhishek Aggarwal (MeitY), Avinash Agarwal (DoT, Invitee), Shreeppriya Gopalakrishnan (IndiaAI, Convenor). 4 Parts: Principles (7 sutras), Issues & Recommendations (6 pillars), Action Plan, Practical Guidelines for Industry & Regulators. Proposes new institutions: AI Governance Group (AIGG), AI Safety Institute (AISI), Technology & Policy Expert Committee (TPEC). Adapts principles from RBI FREE-AI Committee report. Hosted at static.pib.gov.in; landing at meity.gov.in/india-ai-governance-guidelines.
authorities: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
rows.authority: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

rows.title: Nigeria NITDA National Cloud Policy 2025
rows.summary: NITDA National Cloud Policy (NCP2025) — National Sovereign Cloud Initiative. Mandates sensitive data (finance, healthcare, government) be stored within Nigeria. Requires public cloud / primary data centres / Designated Availability Zones to be resident in Nigeria.

rows.title: Philippines BSP Economic Newsletter EN25-01 — Adopting Generative AI in Philippine IT-BPM
rows.summary: BSP Economic Newsletter No. 25-01 (July 2025) by Caynila and Centeno. Sectoral analysis of generative-AI opportunities and risks in Philippine IT-BPM industry, framing the BSP's research outlook on AI in financial-services-adjacent industries. Topics: AI, GenAI, sectoral economics. Non-binding research.

rows.summary: We are calling on all manufacturers and developers of smart products to prioritise people’s privacy with new guidance published today (16 June).

rows.summary: NPC and Insurance Commission joint advisory (11 March 2025) on Considerations on the Use of Privacy Enhancing Technologies (PETs) in the Insurance Industry. Non-binding but informs how regulators assess compliance with accountability and security obligations under the Data Privacy Act. PDF blocked by Cloudflare for fetch; metadata per AI Note B6 + B10. Topics: Privacy, AI, sectoral.

rows.title: Philippines CPBRD Discussion Paper DP2025-02 — Global Trends in the Regulation and Governance of AI
rows.summary: Congressional Policy and Budget Research Department (CPBRD) Discussion Paper DP2025-02 (February 2025) surveying international approaches to AI regulation. Informs ongoing legislative deliberations including HB 13/1920/2827/3195/etc. and the House ICT Committee Technical Working Group on the AI Development and Regulation Act. Topics: AI, comparative regulation, legislative research. PDF blocked by Cloudflare for fetch; metadata per AI Note B10.

rows.title: Philippines DOST PCIEERD Artificial Intelligence Roadmap (December 2024)
rows.summary: DOST AI Roadmap (December 2024) outlining national priorities and strategic directions for AI development and adoption. Non-binding policy document. Topics: AI, national strategy. PDF blocked by Cloudflare for fetch; metadata per AI Note B10.
authorities: Philippines Department of Science and Technology
rows.authority: Philippines Department of Science and Technology

rows.title: Nigeria CBN Risk-Based Cybersecurity Framework and Guidelines for Deposit Money Banks and Payment Service Banks 2024
rows.summary: CBN risk-based cybersecurity framework. Issued 31 May 2024; effective 1 July 2024. Applies to commercial, merchant, non-interest and payment service banks. Seven parts: cybersecurity governance, risk management, resilience, emerging technologies, metrics, monitoring & reporting, compliance. Aligns with BOFIA 2020 and NDPA 2023.

rows.title: Philippines DICT-CSC Draft Joint Memorandum Circular on Ethical and Trustworthy AI in Government
rows.summary: Draft Joint Memorandum Circular (April 18, 2024) by DICT and CSC setting principles for fairness, accountability, and transparency in government AI use. In draft form since April 2024; not formally adopted. Topics: AI, government policy. PDF blocked by Cloudflare for fetch; metadata per AI Note B10.
authorities: Philippines Department of Information and Communications Technology
rows.authority: Philippines Department of Information and Communications Technology

rows.title: Nigeria NDPC Guidance Notice on Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors of Major Importance 2024
rows.summary: NDPC guidance on determining whether entities qualify as Data Controllers or Data Processors of Major Importance (DCPMI) under the NDPA. First issued 14 February 2024; updated and incorporated into the GAID 2025.

rows.title: Philippines BSP Project Sapiens — Thematic Review on AI/ML in Financial Services
rows.summary: BSP Technology Risk and Innovation Supervision Department thematic review (2024) — Project Sapiens — surveying use of AI and machine learning in Philippine financial services. Identifies risks (data privacy, security, ethical) and supervisory expectations. Topics: AI, ML, financial supervision. Non-binding supervisory research.

rows.title: Philippines BSP Economic Newsletter EN21-03 — Thinking AI-Head: ML Applications in Central Banks
rows.summary: BSP Economic Newsletter No. 21-03 (December 2021) by Amodia, Gabriel, and Mapa. Research piece exploring ML applications in central banks — early scene-setting for the BSP's subsequent regulatory engagement with AI/ML. Topics: AI, financial supervision, central banking. Non-binding research.

rows.title: Nigeria ONSA National Cybersecurity Policy and Strategy 2021
rows.summary: National Cybersecurity Policy and Strategy launched by President Buhari on 23 February 2021. Issued under § 41(b) of the Cybercrimes Act 2015. Covers cybercrime, cyber-espionage, cyber-conflict, cyber-terrorism, child online abuse. Implemented through ONSA's National Cybersecurity Coordination Centre (NCCC).
authorities: Nigeria Office of the National Security Adviser
rows.authority: Nigeria Office of the National Security Adviser

rows.title: Nigeria ONSA Action Plan for Implementation of the National Cybersecurity Strategy
rows.summary: ONSA draft Action Plan operationalising the National Cybersecurity Strategy. Decomposes the Strategy's seven thematic areas into deliverables with KPIs and timelines: legal/regulatory framework, national incident management, CII protection, cyber awareness and child online protection, capacity building, governance.
authorities: Nigeria Office of the National Security Adviser
